label: Warm Editorial
description: Broader content-first variant for publishing, CMS-led sites, and service storytelling.
signatureMotif: content-first rhythm, warm editorial hierarchy, and calm section flow
promptHints: Organize the experience around storytelling and reading cadence rather than conversion-only stacking.
Favor rich content hierarchy, callouts, and believable editorial structure.

layouts: Use a magazine-style homepage with a featured lead story spanning two columns beside a stacked list of secondary articles.
Build article pages with a narrow reading column, generous side margins, and a right-rail for pull quotes or section navigation.
Use a sectioned content hub with alternating editorial bands: intro copy, curated story grid, expert callout, then newsletter block.
Compose service storytelling pages as long-form narratives with chapter-like sections, inline testimonials, and a soft CTA near the end.
Use author or category archives with a prominent intro header followed by a masonry-like card rhythm that still preserves clear reading order.
motifs: Pair warm paper-like backgrounds with dark ink text, muted beige separators, and terracotta accents that appear sparingly on interactive elements.
Use serif headlines over clean sans body copy, with oversized editorial spacing and clear typographic contrast between feature stories and supporting content.
Show cards and callouts as soft surfaced panels with 1rem radius, thin low-contrast borders, and almost no heavy shadow.
Introduce calm hierarchy through eyebrow labels, pull quotes, divider rules, and captioned imagery that make pages feel published rather than marketed.
antiPatterns: Avoid startup-style landing stacks with repetitive feature blocks and aggressive CTA repetition that interrupts the reading cadence.
Never use glossy gradients, neon accents, or glassmorphism panels; they break the quiet editorial warmth of the variant.
Avoid ultra-dense dashboard grids or cramped multi-column text layouts that make the experience feel utilitarian instead of readable.
Do not make every section full-bleed and visually loud; this variant needs measured margins and breathing room between story beats.
